input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the relevant aerodynamic devices are highlighted in pink. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.7.5.a specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.7.5
            TITLE: length

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.5: Length
T.7.5 Length 
In plan view, any part of any Aerodynamic Device must be:  
a. No more than 700 mm forward of the fronts of the front tires 
b. No more than 250 mm rearward of the rear of the rear tires

final_answer:
Explanation:The rule T.7.5.a requires two specific measurements: the distance from the front-most point of the aerodynamic device to the front tires must be no more than 700 mm, and the distance from the rear-most point of the aerodynamic device to the rear tires must be no more than 250 mm. The engineering drawing explicitly provides the overall length of the vehicle (705.28 mm), but it does not explicitly provide the required dimensions for the forward or rearward extensions relative to the tires. Since the specific dimensions needed to verify compliance with both parts of rule T.7.5.a are not explicitly shown, compliance must be assumed.
Answer:Yes
